services.name: University Hospital Waterford (UHW)
services.notes: Main regional hospital for the Southeast. Emergency department, surgical services, oncology, cardiology. Part of South/South West Hospital Group. Public and private patients. Long ED wait times common.

services.name: Boots Pharmacy Waterford
services.notes: Full-service pharmacy. Flu vaccinations, prescription dispensing, health consultations. Open Mon-Sat. Multiple Boots and LloydsPharmacy locations in Waterford.

services.name: AIB (Allied Irish Banks) Waterford
services.notes: Full-service branch. Current accounts, savings, mortgages. English-speaking (native). Opening an account requires proof of address and PPS number.

services.name: Bank of Ireland Waterford
services.notes: Full-service branch. Largest bank in Ireland. Current accounts for new residents available with proof of ID and address.

services.name: Permanent TSB (PTSB) Waterford
services.notes: Retail bank with competitive current account offerings. Fewer branches than AIB/BOI but good digital banking.

services.name: An Post Waterford
services.notes: Main post office. An Post handles mail, parcels, bill payments, foreign exchange, and some government services. Eircode (postal code) system used for addresses.

services.name: FLAC (Free Legal Advice Centres)
services.notes: Free legal advice clinics held at Citizens Information Centres. Covers immigration, housing, employment, consumer rights. Volunteer solicitors. Appointment required.

services.name: Citizens Information Waterford
services.notes: Free information, advice, and advocacy on public services and entitlements. Covers social welfare, healthcare, immigration, consumer rights. Walk-in and phone service.

services.name: Waterford City GP Practices
services.notes: Multiple GP practices in Waterford. Consultation €55-65 per visit. Over-70s GP visit card covers visits free. Register with a GP for ongoing care. Healthlink and online booking available.

services.name: Waterford Veterinary Hospital
services.notes: Full-service veterinary hospital. Emergency care, surgery, dental, diagnostics. Multiple smaller vet clinics in Waterford city and surrounding towns.

services.name: Tramore Veterinary Clinic
services.notes: Small animal veterinary clinic serving Tramore and surrounding coastal areas. Routine care, vaccinations, microchipping.

sources.title: Veterinary Council of Ireland - Find a Vet

services.name: People's Park Waterford
services.notes: Victorian-era public park in city centre. Dogs allowed on leash. Playground, bandstand, walking paths. Popular with dog walkers.

services.name: WIT Arena (SETU Arena)
services.notes: Modern sports and fitness facility at South East Technological University. Gym, swimming pool, group fitness classes. Public membership available.

services.name: Waterford Airport (WAT)
services.notes: Small regional airport. Limited scheduled services. Cork Airport (120km) and Dublin Airport (170km) offer full international connections.

services.name: Plunkett Station (Waterford Rail)
services.notes: Irish Rail station. Direct service to Dublin Heuston (2hr 10min), Limerick Junction, and Rosslare Europort (ferry to Wales/France). Bus Éireann services from adjacent bus station.

services.name: Tesco
services.notes: Mainstream supermarket chain.

services.name: Aldi
services.notes: German discount grocer — widespread in Ireland.

services.name: Currys
services.notes: Electronics + appliances chain.

services.name: Woodies
services.notes: Home-improvement chain.

services.name: Penneys / Primark
services.notes: Women's clothing — Ireland chain / directory.

services.name: Dunnes Stores
services.notes: Men's clothing — Ireland chain / directory.

services.name: Dunnes Stores Kids
services.notes: Children's clothing — Ireland chain / directory.

services.name: Marks & Spencer
services.notes: Big & Tall clothing — Ireland chain / directory.

attractions.name: House of Waterford Crystal
attractions.description: World-famous crystal factory tour and retail store. Watch master craftsmen create hand-cut crystal.

attractions.name: Viking Triangle & Reginald's Tower
attractions.description: Ireland's oldest civic building (1003 AD) and surrounding Viking-era quarter with three museums: Medieval Museum, Bishop's Palace, and Reginald's Tower.
attractions.frequency: Open daily except some winter closures

attractions.name: Waterford Greenway
attractions.description: 46km off-road cycling and walking trail from Waterford city to Dungarvan, following the old railway line through countryside and coast
attractions.frequency: Open daily, year-round. Bike rental available at multiple points.

attractions.name: Dunmore East Cliffs & Harbour
attractions.description: Dramatic coastal cliffs, secluded coves, and a working fishing harbour. Cliff walks with sea views.

attractions.name: Copper Coast UNESCO Global Geopark
attractions.description: 17km stretch of dramatic coastline between Tramore and Dungarvan with 460-million-year geological formations, sea stacks, and coastal walks
attractions.frequency: Open year-round. Geopark Centre in Bunmahon.

attractions.name: Theatre Royal Waterford
attractions.description: Beautifully restored 19th-century theatre hosting drama, comedy, music, and cultural events throughout the year

attractions.name: Waterford Food & Pub Scene
attractions.description: Traditional Irish pubs with live trad music sessions, craft beer scene, and strong local food culture. Notable restaurants in city and Dungarvan.

attractions.name: Walsh Park (GAA)
attractions.description: Home ground for Waterford GAA hurling and football. Hurling is the dominant sport in Waterford — fast-paced and uniquely Irish.
attractions.frequency: Match days, Feb-Sep season (hurling championship peaks in summer)
